subject: environmental_science
educational_personality: exceptional classroom teacher; cream textbook; diagram teaches

lesson.big_idea: Waste management reduces harm by refusing, reducing, reusing, and recycling.

sections.title: Have You Wondered?
sections.body: Where does yesterday's plastic bottle go if we toss it carelessly? Waste choices travel farther than we see.

sections.title: Using the Diagram
sections.body: Follow Refuse → Reduce → Reuse → Recycle. The diagram prioritises prevention before recycling.

sections.title: Concept: Waste Hierarchy
sections.body: The waste hierarchy ranks actions. Preventing waste beats recycling something that did not need to exist.

sections.title: Say It Simply
sections.body: Best first: don't create waste. Then use less, use again, and recycle what remains.

sections.title: In Real Life
sections.body: Carrying a refillable bottle refuses a new disposable bottle before recycling is even needed.

sections.title: Worked Example
sections.body: Sort a lunch scrap pile: food to compost, clean paper to recycle, and a broken toy to repair or reuse.

sections.title: Try This
sections.body: Point to Reduce and give one school action that creates less waste.

sections.title: Lesson Summary
sections.body: Smart waste habits follow a hierarchy. Prevention and reuse protect environments better than last-minute recycling alone.

sections.title: Think About It
sections.body: Which bin at home is fullest, and what does that reveal about your week's choices?

sections.title: Apply Your Learning
sections.body: Design a classroom poster that uses the four diagram steps in the correct order.

diagram_package.title: Waste and Recycling
diagram_package.caption: Refuse → Reduce → Reuse → Recycle
diagram_package.explanation: This diagram teaches how the ideas in Waste and Recycling connect.
diagram_package.practice_question: Point to Recycle and explain why it is not the first choice.
